name: TitleMax Title Loans
description_short: TitleMax offers same-day title pledge loans and flex lines of credit using your vehicle as collateral, accepting most credit types at retail storefronts across the South and Midwest.
description_long:
TitleMax is one of the largest title lending chains in the United States, operating retail storefronts across states including Tennessee, Texas, Missouri, Mississippi, South Carolina, and others. The Memphis Summer Avenue location has been operating since February 2007, making it a long-established presence in that market. The company is headquartered outside of Tennessee and operates as a consumer finance lender focused on short-term secured lending to borrowers who may not qualify for traditional bank products.

The core product is a title pledge loan — a secured loan where the borrower hands over the vehicle title (while keeping the car) in exchange for a lump-sum cash advance. This Memphis location also offers a Flex Line of Credit, a revolving secured credit line that allows customers to draw funds over time up to an approved limit rather than taking a single lump sum. In addition, TitleMax has expanded into unsecured flexible lines of credit, available both in-store and online for residents of eligible states (AZ, DE, ID, KS, MO, MS, SC, TN, TX, UT, WI). These unsecured products require an active bank account and proof of income but do not require a vehicle title.

What distinguishes TitleMax within the title lending space is the combination of secured and unsecured product options under one roof, bilingual staff at this location, and extended weekday hours (open until 7 pm on Mondays and Fridays). The flex line structure offers more repayment flexibility than a traditional single-installment title loan, which is a meaningful difference for borrowers managing irregular income. The Google review average of 5.0 across 481+ reviews at this location reflects consistently positive frontline service experiences.

Honestly, title loans — regardless of lender — are among the highest-cost financial products available to consumers. TitleMax's website does not disclose APR, fee structures, or typical loan amounts on the store page, which makes direct cost comparison difficult before applying. Vehicle repossession is a real risk if repayment fails, and the flex line structure, while more flexible, can extend indebtedness. Borrowers should exhaust lower-cost alternatives — credit union payday alternative loans, employer advances, or nonprofit credit counseling — before turning to title lending.
address: 1331 Church St Ste A, Georgetown, SC 29440

company_info.headquarters: 1331 Church St Ste A, Georgetown, SC 29440

pros: Same-day cash funding with quick in-store approval process
Vehicle title secures the loan, so most credit types are accepted regardless of credit score
Borrower keeps and continues driving their vehicle during the loan term
Flex Line of Credit option allows withdrawals over time rather than forcing a lump-sum draw
Both secured (title pledge) and unsecured line of credit products available at the same location
Bilingual staff on-site at this Memphis location
Long-established location operating since February 2007 with 481+ Google reviews averaging 5.0
cons: No APR, fee schedule, or typical loan cost disclosed on the website — borrowers cannot comparison-shop before applying
Vehicle repossession is a real consequence of default on title pledge loans
Online unsecured line of credit is restricted to 11 states; not available nationally
Store is closed Sundays, limiting access during weekend emergencies
Title loans are a high-cost product class; TitleMax does not disclose rates that would allow evaluation against alternatives
best_for: Vehicle owners with poor or no credit who need fast cash and have exhausted lower-cost options
Memphis-area residents needing same-day funding without a strong credit profile
Borrowers who prefer a revolving credit line over a single lump-sum loan
Spanish-speaking borrowers in the Memphis area who need bilingual service
diagnosis: TitleMax is best suited for vehicle-owning borrowers with damaged or limited credit who need same-day cash and have no access to credit unions, employer advances, or other sub-36% APR alternatives. The main caveat is significant: title loans carry very high costs and the risk of vehicle loss on default, and TitleMax does not publish its rates upfront, making it impossible to evaluate cost before applying.
services: Car title pledge loans (vehicle as collateral)
Unsecured flexible line of credit (online, 11 states)
Loan repayment management via customer portal
